title: LetGo / Conductor Film Platform
thesis_claim: LetGo stages a distributed authorship model where timeline authority is centralized but meaning emerges from synchronized multi-device participation.
cybernetic_loop: Swift harness emits cues, backend distributes and logs state, phone clients drift-correct and respond, telemetry returns to the conductor for live adaptation.
material_system: Swift harness, Fastify/WebSocket backend, React phone client, shared protocol package, rehearsal replay store.
agency_model: Constrained collective agency: deterministic spine with dynamic overlays and per-device variant behavior.

evidence_refs.label: Runtime Topology
evidence_refs.excerpt: A monorepo for a conducted confessional cinema system split across one projector surface and a field of NFC-identified phone participants.

evidence_refs.label: Architecture Notes
evidence_refs.excerpt: Swift harness emits CueCommand to backend; backend stamps and fan-outs cue stream; phone clients estimate drift and bias playback clock.

evidence_refs.label: Harness Runtime
evidence_refs.excerpt: ShowStateMachine drives deterministic fixed timeline transitions with command/vector dispatch and replay tooling.

risk_notes: Network instability can break liveness unless replay/fallback pathways are primed.
Operator cognitive load rises if command surface is not constrained.
